---
# Parse a pipe-delimited composite field and aggregate the numeric part

**Dataset**: `guardian_xql_lab` (composite/array lookup lab)

A single text column packs several values behind a delimiter (`login_combo = "user|host|action|count"`). `split(col, "|")` turns it into an array, `arrayindex(arr, N)` pulls each 0-based position (use `arrayindex(arr, -1)` for the last when arity varies), and `to_number(...)` casts the numeric part so it can be summed. Lead with the split, project the parts, then aggregate.

```sql
dataset = guardian_xql_lab
| alter parts = split(login_combo, "|")
| alter cu_user = arrayindex(parts, 0), cu_host = arrayindex(parts, 1), cu_action = arrayindex(parts, 2), cu_cnt = to_number(arrayindex(parts, 3))
| comp sum(cu_cnt) as total by cu_action
| sort desc total
```

_Live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant; measured cost ~0.0003 CU against a 10-row lookup (data scanned, not query complexity, drives CU)._
